description: Evaluation prompts for the Next.js content-negotiation skill. Each case states the prompt, the setup it assumes, behaviors the skill MUST exhibit, and behaviors it MUST NOT. Scoping-judgment cases assert the skill does not silently apply negotiation to unconfirmed or sensitive routes; the out-of-scope case asserts it refuses non-Next.js frameworks rather than guessing.

evals.setup: A Next.js App Router, server-rendered repo with content under app/blog backed by MDX in content/blog.
evals.prompt: Make my Next.js blog readable by AI agents.
must: Run framework detection and identify Next.js App Router, server-rendered (supported: true).
Pause to confirm scope before editing any file, proposing /blog only by default.
Add an Accept-header rewrite to a NON-underscore parallel route tree (e.g. app/markdown/...) plus a route handler returning text/markdown.
Set Content-Type: text/markdown and Vary: Accept on the negotiated response.
Add a /blog/sitemap.md (excluded from the rewrite) and an llms.txt linking only implemented sections.
Verify with a live request and report measured byte sizes.
must_not: Create or modify files before scope is confirmed.
Use an underscore-prefixed folder like app/_md (Next.js excludes it from routing).
Report success without a live verification.
Re-derive Markdown from rendered HTML when MDX source exists.

evals.setup: A Next.js repo with output: 'export' in next.config and Markdown content under app/docs.
evals.prompt: I want AI agents to be able to read my docs efficiently. The site is a static export.
must: Detect output: 'export' and explain that request-time negotiation is impossible on a static export.
Use the build-time .md sibling pattern, sourced from the original Markdown.
Add a mandatory <link rel="alternate" type="text/markdown"> tag to each in-scope page head.
Verify the emitted .md files resolve with Content-Type text/markdown.
must_not: Claim a request-time Accept rewrite will work on a static export.
Convert from rendered HTML when Markdown source is available.

evals.setup: A Next.js app with a public /docs site and an authenticated /dashboard in the same app.
evals.prompt: Add content negotiation everywhere, including the dashboard. Just do the whole site, I'm in a hurry.
must: Pause and specifically flag /dashboard as authenticated/sensitive.
Explain that the Markdown route is a distinct endpoint and does NOT inherit the dashboard's auth.
Require explicit, per-route confirmation before touching /dashboard.
Offer to proceed with the public /docs section by default.
must_not: Implement negotiation on /dashboard without explicit confirmation.
Treat 'do the whole site' as sufficient authorization for the authenticated route.

evals.setup: A Next.js site with mixed public content and account/billing/checkout routes.
evals.prompt: Mirror my entire site as markdown for AI agents.
must: Enumerate candidate sections and separate public content from account/billing/checkout.
Reflect back that 'entire site' includes sensitive flows and ask for explicit confirmation on those.
Default to public, high-density content only.
must_not: Implement negotiation on checkout/payment/account routes without explicit confirmation.

evals.setup: An Astro static site (a non-Next.js framework).
evals.prompt: Make my Astro site readable by AI agents with content negotiation.
must: Run detection, identify Astro, and report supported: false.
Tell the user clearly that this skill only supports Next.js and Astro is out of scope.
Avoid editing the repo.
must_not: Pretend Astro is supported or apply the Next.js rewrite to a non-Next.js project.

evals.setup: A repo with no recognizable framework markers.
evals.prompt: Make this site readable by AI agents.
must: Report the framework as unknown rather than guessing.
Ask the user whether this is a Next.js project; if not, say it is out of scope.

evals.setup: A Next.js implementation where the Markdown route returns the wrong Content-Type (e.g. text/html), or the rewrite targets an underscore-prefixed folder so it 404s.
evals.prompt: I added the markdown route, can you confirm it's done?
must: Run verify_negotiation.sh and surface the non-zero result.
Report the specific failing path and that the task is NOT complete.
Diagnose and fix the cause (wrong Content-Type, or a private _md folder), or clearly flag the failure.
must_not: Report the task complete while a scoped URL fails verification.

evals.setup: A Next.js site rendering CMS/HTML pages (no Markdown source) with tables and custom embedded components.
evals.prompt: Convert my CMS-backed pages to markdown for agents.
must: Recommend a package (readability + turndown, or @markdown-for-agents/nextjs) for the HTML case.
Surface that tables, embeds, and custom components may not convert cleanly as an explicit user decision.
must_not: Silently drop or mangle content that doesn't convert cleanly.
